# Attention — Tokens Talking to Each Other {#sec-ch06-attention}

> _"Every token asks: 'Who here is relevant to me?' Every other token answers. The answer is a weighted blend of their values."_

Attention is the engine of the transformer.
It is the mechanism that lets each token look at all (or some) of the other tokens and decide which ones to borrow information from.
Without attention, each token would be processed independently, oblivious to context.
With attention, the word "bank" can tell whether the surrounding tokens are about rivers or finance.

This chapter builds scaled dot-product attention from first principles.

{{< include book/06-attention/sections/idea.qmd >}}

{{< include book/06-attention/sections/math.qmd >}}

{{< include book/06-attention/sections/matrix.qmd >}}

{{< include book/06-attention/sections/code.qmd >}}

{{< include book/06-attention/sections/takeaways.qmd >}}
